entry.title: Removal of legacy sap.ui.webc.* Web Component UI libraries from SAPUI5/OpenUI5 1.147.0 onwards
entry.content:
Saying Goodbye to sap.ui.webc.* — How SAPUI5/OpenUI5 Now Integrates Web Components Natively

If you've used the sap.ui.webc.main, sap.ui.webc.fiori, or sap.ui.webc.common libraries in a SAPUI5 or OpenUI5 application, this post is for you. These libraries will be removed in version 1.147.0, one release before the next long-term support (LTS) version 1.148. They will not be available in the 1.148 LTS or any version after.

Here's what happened, what's changing, and what you should do.

The Short Version

The sap.ui.webc.* libraries were an experimental attempt to bring UI5 Web Components into the SAPUI5 programming model. They were introduced in version 1.92 (2021) as experimental, deprecated in version 1.120 (2023), and will be removed in version 1.147.0. They never left experimental status.

The new solution is a much better approach: native Web Components integration built directly into sap.ui.core, available since version 1.138. Instead of frozen wrapper libraries, you now consume Web Components from npm as first-class citizens of the framework.

What Were the sap.ui.webc.* Libraries?

The idea was straightforward: take each UI5 Web Component (like ui5-button, ui5-shellbar, ui5-table) and wrap it as a classic SAPUI5 ManagedObject-based control — complete with properties, aggregations, events, and data binding support.

Three libraries made up this approach:

Library Purpose

sap.ui.webc.commonBase infrastructure, bundled the UI5 Web Components runtime
sap.ui.webc.main~70 controls wrapping @ui5/webcomponents (Button, Input, Table, Dialog, ...)
sap.ui.webc.fiori~30 controls wrapping @ui5/webcomponents-fiori (ShellBar, Wizard, Timeline, ...)

This "retrofit" approach worked, but it came with significant drawbacks:

  • Frozen dependency. The libraries bundled @ui5/webcomponents v1.18.0 as static thirdparty files. When the UI5 Web Components project shipped v2.x with major improvements, the sap.ui.webc.* libraries stayed on v1.18 — forever.
  • Maintenance overhead. Every new Web Component or API change required manually updating the wrapper code and re-bundling the thirdparty files. This didn't scale.
  • Experimental lock. Because of these constraints, the libraries never graduated beyond @experimental status. They were always flagged with: "This API is experimental and might change significantly."

Why They Were Deprecated

In version 1.120 — the previous LTS release — all three libraries were marked @deprecated with the message:

"This Web Components consumption model has been discarded. An alternative consumption model is planned for the future."

That alternative has arrived a while ago.

The Better Approach: sap.ui.core.webc.WebComponent

Since version 1.138, SAPUI5 includes a new public base class — sap.ui.core.webc.WebComponent — that lives directly in sap.ui.core. This is not another wrapper library. It's a framework-level integration point.

Here's what's different:

1. Web Components come from npm, not from frozen bundles.

Instead of shipping a static copy of @ui5/webcomponents, the new approach uses ui5-tooling-modules to consume Web Components directly from npm packages at build time. This means your application stays on current Web Component versions — today v2.x — rather than being locked to a frozen v1.18 copy.

2. Integration is at the framework level.

The WebComponent base class in sap.ui.core handles property propagation, aggregation-to-slot mapping, event forwarding, and rendering integration generically. Individual Web Component wrappers are thin — generated metadata, not hand-maintained control code.

3. The framework is already using it internally.

The new integration approach is already used within the framework itself — for example, the Shell and Enterprise Search in the sap.f library consume Web Components through this mechanism. While these specific integrations are currently private/restricted APIs, they validate that the approach works at scale in production scenarios. Public Web Component-based controls built on sap.ui.core.webc.WebComponent will follow.

4. It's public API.

Unlike the sap.ui.webc.* libraries which stayed experimental for their entire lifecycle, sap.ui.core.webc.WebComponent is a public, stable API since 1.138.

What You Should Do

If you never used sap.ui.webc.*: Nothing changes for you. If you want to use Web Components in your SAPUI5 app going forward, read the Using Web Components documentation.

If you used sap.ui.webc.* in a prototype or experiment: Since these libraries were always experimental, now is the time to move to the new approach. The controls you were using (like sap.ui.webc.main.Button or sap.ui.webc.fiori.ShellBar) have no 1:1 migration path because the consumption model changed fundamentally. However, the underlying Web Components are the same — and better, since you now get v2.x instead of v1.18.

If you used sap.ui.webc.* in a productive application despite the experimental status: The libraries will be removed in version 1.147.0. We recommend starting the migration now. The Using Web Components guide is your starting point.

If you're on the 1.120 LTS and planning to upgrade to the next LTS: The previous LTS (1.120) still ships the deprecated sap.ui.webc.* libraries. The next LTS (1.148) will not — they are removed one version earlier in 1.147. If your application depends on these libraries, you must migrate before upgrading to 1.148 LTS.

Timeline

Version Event

1.92 (2021)sap.ui.webc.* introduced as experimental
1.120 (2023)sap.ui.webc.* deprecated — previous LTS
1.138 (2025)sap.ui.core.webc.WebComponent introduced as public API
1.147sap.ui.webc.* libraries removed
1.148Next LTS release — sap.ui.webc.* not available

Summary

The sap.ui.webc.* libraries were an early experiment that taught us a lot about what Web Components integration in SAPUI5 should look like. The answer turned out to be: don't bundle frozen copies — integrate at the framework level and let developers consume Web Components from the ecosystem directly.

That's exactly what the new approach does, and it's available today.

Next step: Using Web Components in SAPUI5

entry.title: Re: In SAP S/4 HANA Public Cloud Can We Create ODN Number...
entry.content:
Hello Ashish,

The Number ranges in S/4HANA Public Cloud are typically determined by : Document type (e.g., delivery type like LF) and Number range object configuration is defined based on the Document Type. They do NOT support dynamic criteria like Sold-To Party.

Hope this helps!!

Thanks & Regards,
Vivek Parasuraman.

entry.title: Re: RFC Adapter on Edge Integration Cell
entry.content:
Hello 

great blog. I tried out also to setup SNC but this only works with setting the additional parameter

jco.client.snc_sso = 0

This is per default 1 which means the SNC identity is used for authentication.

0 means the username/password logon and SNC is used only for encryption of the connection.

This then works out for me (with username/password logon).

With setting the parameter (which is the standard value if you do not set it at all)

jco.client.snc_sso = 1

it does not work for me.
I have opened a ticket at SAP and the answer was as follows:

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------

The issue (Dump in ABAP-System CALL_FUNCTION_SIGNON_INCOMPL) is caused by enabling SNC SSO (jco.client.snc_sso = 1) without a complete SSO setup. 

With SNC SSO enabled, the connection requires authentication via SNC identity (e.g. certificates). In this mode, username/password is ignored. Since no SNC identity (such as snc_myname) and no corresponding SAP user mapping are configured, the logon fails with CALL_FUNCTION_SIGNON_INCOMPL.

Using jco.client.snc_sso = 0 is the correct configuration in this case. It enables secure SNC encryption while authenticating via username/password, which is fully supported and working as expected.

If you would still like to proceed, the SNC SSO should use a technical identity (e.g. via client certificate/PSE and SNC name mapping), but this scenario is not officially supported and has not been tested for the current available implementation.

The following setup could be considered but this setup is not guaranteed to work, as it is not officially supported or intended for supported use:

  • Configure a client certificate / PSE on the destination
  • Set:
    • jco.client.snc_myname = p:CN=<TECH_USER>
    • jco.client.snc_sso = 1

On the ABAP side:

  • Maintain the corresponding SNC name in transaction SU01:
    • SNC name = p:CN=<TECH_USER>

----------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Did you or anybody got this working with jco.client.snc_sso = 1besides SAP mentioned that this is not officially supported??

Regards
Sebastian

entry.title: SAP Public Cloud Partner Advisory Council-Sales: Driving Better Collaboration and Product Usability
entry.content:
SAP Public Cloud Partner Advisory Council
Making collaboration more direct. Making products more usable.

As more customers adopt SAP Public Cloud, partners play an increasingly critical role in sales, delivery, and customer success.

At the same time, we have consistently heard feedback from our partners:

  • The need for earlier visibility into SAP’s roadmap
  • Better clarity on what capabilities will be delivered and when
  • A more direct way to bring real project requirements into product discussions

To address these needs, SAP Public Cloud Sales has launched the SAP Public Cloud Partner Advisory Council (PAC).

Why PAC?

Traditionally, partner feedback has been collected through channels such as WCR and CIP.

While these mechanisms are important for structured feedback, we have observed some limitations:

  • Feedback cycles can be relatively long, making it difficult to respond quickly to real project needs
  • Inputs are often based on individual requirements rather than broader business scenarios
  • The process of how feedback influences the product roadmap is not always transparent

PAC is designed to complement these channels by providing a more direct and interactive way to collaborate.

Our goal is simple: enable faster communication, clearer alignment, and improved product usability.

What we do in PAC

PAC focuses on real product topics and real project challenges.

Roadmap sharing
We share the latest roadmap updates for SAP Public Cloud (e.g. S/4HANA Cloud, BTP), including planned and under-evaluation capabilities, and explain their impact on sales and project delivery.

Real-time discussion and Q&A
We collect partner questions in advance, discuss key topics during the session, and define clear follow-up actions where needed.

Direct connection with product teams
PAC enables direct interaction between partners and product teams, helping ensure that real business scenarios are better reflected in product design.

Real use cases and experience sharing
Partners are encouraged to share project experiences, integration and extensibility scenarios, as well as sales and delivery insights.

What we have discussed so far

PAC sessions are built around real agendas.

Topics from previous sessions include:

  • SAP Cloud ERP for Sales strategy and roadmap
  • Fiori roadmap (e.g. 2602 / 2608) with system demos
  • Manage Sales Documents scenarios
  • Extensibility and UI use cases
  • Custom Sales Order Type
  • Partner Success Stories and Customer Stories
  • Product surveys, user research, and partner feedback

Each session also includes dedicated time for open discussion, Q&A, and clear next steps.

A continuously evolving format

PAC is not a one-time event.

We continuously shape future PAC sessions based on the topics partners are most interested in. This ensures that discussions remain relevant and that feedback can effectively influence product improvements.

What partners gain

By participating in PAC, partners can:

  • Better understand product direction and roadmap
  • Reduce uncertainty in sales and project delivery
  • Bring requirements more directly into product discussions
  • Learn from other partners’ real-world experiences

How to join

The SAP Public Cloud Partner Advisory Council is invitation-based.

To ensure open and productive discussions, participating partners are required to sign an SAP FBA (Feedback Agreement).

If you are interested in joining PAC, please contact:

Bernie.xiong@sap.com

We will follow up with further details and invitations.

entry.title: 如何为 SAP Universal ID 添加或删除附加账号与邮箱
entry.content:
如果你已有 SAP Universal ID，可以在 SAP 账号管理（SAP Account Manager）中添加额外的邮箱，并在必要时将相关的 S-/P-user  进行关联绑定。这样一来，你就能用同一 SAP Universal ID 管理多个邮箱，并将企业或个人的 S/P 用户账号链接起来，便于管理。 

 

具体步骤如下：  

  1. 进入SAP Account Manager: https://account.sap.com/ 
  2. 点击界面右上角 "Add Identity"  

   3. 输入需绑定S/P账号关联的邮箱，再点击提交 

 

      4.输入已发送至您邮箱的验证码以确认提交。 

 

 

    5.点击"Verify"，成功绑定S/P 账号 

 

 

每个电子邮件地址（无论是否关联 S-/P-User）仅可绑定到一个 SAP Universal ID。若该邮箱已隶属于其他 SAP Universal ID，则无法将其添加至本账户；且 SAP Universal ID 之间不支持合并。若需将该邮箱绑定到当前 SAP  Universal ID，请先从原 SAP  Universal ID 中移除该邮箱，或删除重复的 SAP  Universal ID 

 

下面给您介绍如何移除邮箱和账号： 

  1. 登录SAP Account Manager. 

 

 

     2.在“Identities”一栏中找到并选中要解除关联的账号 

 

    3.点击页面右上角的“三个点”图标，选择“解除身份关联（Unlink Identity）” 选项。 

 

 

 

     4.点击"Confirm"完成解绑 

 

 

请注意： 

只有在同一 SAP Universal ID 已关联多个账号的情况下，用户才可通过自助服务（Self Service）解除某一账号的关联。如果某个账号是与该 SAP Universal ID关联的唯一账号，则不会显示“解除身份关联（Unlink Identity）”选项，此时仅可由 SAP 支持团队代为解除关联。 

 

如果需要绑定的邮箱已经是SAP Universal ID 且没有证书绑定的情况下，可以申请删除该SAP Universal ID, 然后再将此邮箱绑定到另外的SAP Universal ID 上面 

 

删除SAP Universal ID 方法如下： 

方法一： 在屏幕左侧点击“隐私”，然后点击“请求删除账户”（参见 https://account.sap.com/manage/privacy）。 
您的请求将由客户互动中心（Customer Interaction Center，CIC）处理，处理需要一些时间。如果您需要提前与我们联系，请按照 KBA 560499（Customer Interaction Center：热线 - 电子邮件 - 聊天）中的说明联系客户互动中心，并说明您已提交该请求。 

 

方法二：  

如果您无法登录 SAP Universal ID 账号管理中心，请通过电子邮件向客户互动中心发送请求，并提供以下信息： 

  • SAP Universal ID 的主要电子邮件地址： 
  • 请求原因： 

请注意：电子邮件必须从 SAP Universal ID 的主邮箱发送，或与绑定到该 UID 的账户（S-user ID、P-user ID）关联的邮箱发出 

SAP 支持门户查看所有 CIC 联系渠道的详细信息：https://support.sap.com/en/contact-us.html 

 

方法三： 

您可以创建组件为XX-SER-UID的案例，并提供以下信息： 

  • SAP Universal ID 的主邮箱： 
  • 请求原因： 

请注意：该案例必须由与该 SAP Universal ID 账户关联的某个S-user 创建，或（如果账户所有者联系不上）由该SAP Universal ID 主邮箱隶属客户编号下的超级管理员（super admin）、云管理员（cloud admin）或用户管理员（user admin）创建。

entry.title: SAP 公有云合作伙伴顾问委员会（PAC）— 销售模块
entry.content:
SAP 公有云合作伙伴顾问委员会（PAC）

让合作更直接，让产品更贴近客户

随着中国市场对公有云的监管环境逐步完善，企业上云的障碍正在逐渐减少。SAP 也在持续推进本地化布局，例如通过与阿里云开展战略合作，在中国提供符合合规要求的公有云解决方案。

与此同时，中国中小企业对订阅制 ERP 的接受度正在不断提升，越来越多企业希望通过云化实现更快部署与持续创新。

在这一趋势下，SAP 公有云生态也在不断扩展。除了传统的全球合作伙伴（如 IBM、PwC、德勤等），我们也看到越来越多本土合作伙伴加入到公有云实施与交付中来。

作为最贴近客户的一线伙伴，你们不仅了解客户的真实需求，也理解这些需求背后的业务场景与挑战。

基于这一背景，SAP Public Cloud Sales 推出了 SAP Public Cloud Partner Advisory Council（PAC）。

为什么要设立 PAC？

在日常项目与销售过程中，我们持续听到合作伙伴的反馈：

  • 希望更早了解 SAP 的产品 roadmap
  • 希望更清楚哪些能力会交付、何时交付
  • 希望项目中的真实需求可以更直接进入产品讨论

过去，我们主要通过 WCR 和 CIP 等渠道收集反馈。

这些机制在结构化需求收集方面非常重要，但也存在一些局限：

  • 反馈周期较长，难以及时响应项目中的实际问题
  • 更多基于单点需求，缺少对完整业务场景的讨论
  • 需求如何进入产品 roadmap 的路径不够透明

因此，我们希望建立一种更加直接、高效的沟通方式。

👉 PAC 的目标是：让沟通更直接，让信息更透明，让反馈更有效，从而持续提升产品的可用性。

在 PAC 中，我们会做什么？

PAC 聚焦真实产品、真实场景和真实问题。

Roadmap 分享
分享 SAP Public Cloud（如 S/4HANA Cloud、BTP）的最新 roadmap，并说明对销售与项目交付的影响。

伙伴问题收集与讨论
提前收集项目中的问题，在会议中集中讨论，并明确后续跟进路径。

连接产品团队
邀请产品团队参与讨论，让合作伙伴需求更直接进入产品设计。

案例与经验分享
包括项目实践、集成与扩展场景，以及销售与交付经验。

我们已经讨论过哪些内容？

PAC 的讨论基于真实议程展开，例如：

  • SAP Cloud ERP for Sales 战略与 roadmap
  • Fiori roadmap（2602 / 2608）及系统演示
  • Manage Sales Documents 场景
  • Extensibility 与 UI 使用案例
  • Custom Sales Order Type
  • 伙伴成功案例与客户实践
  • 产品调研、用户研究及伙伴反馈

同时，每次会议都会安排时间用于开放讨论、问答以及明确下一步行动。

持续演进的机制

PAC 不是一次性的会议，而是一个持续优化的机制。

👉 我们会根据合作伙伴最关心的议题，持续设计下一次 PAC 的内容。

这意味着讨论始终贴近实际需求，反馈能够真正影响产品优化。

合作伙伴可以获得什么？

通过参与 PAC，合作伙伴可以：

  • 更早了解产品方向，支持销售决策
  • 更清楚能力落地情况，减少项目不确定性
  • 更直接推动需求进入产品讨论
  • 与其他伙伴交流经验

如何参与？

SAP Public Cloud Partner Advisory Council 采用邀请制。

参与伙伴需要签署 SAP 的 FBA（Feedback Agreement），以支持深入的产品与需求交流。

如果您对参与 PAC 感兴趣，欢迎通过邮件联系我们：

📧 bernie.xiong@sap.com

我们将在确认后发送正式邀请及后续信息。

entry.title: SAP Signavio
entry.content:
Hallo zusammen,

Frage an die Community.

Ich habe bereits x-fach Personen eingeladen einen einzelnen Prozess zu kommentieren,  ohne das diese Personen einen eigenen Account haben. Seit einiger Zeit funktioniert es unregelmäßig nicht und es kommen unterschiedliche Fehlermeldungen.

Bei einigen Personen wird nach Zugangsdaten gefragt, bei anderen kommt direkt eine Fehlermeldung, "Seite nicht aufrufbar".

Ist dieses Probem jemandem bekannt und kann mir einen Hinweis geben, wie ich das abstellen kann?

PS: Prozesse befinden sich noch im Prozess Manager und sind noch nicht im HUB sichtbar, daher Kommentierung ohne Account.

 

Danke für hilfreiche Antworten.

entry.title: CLM - Contract and Lease Management Migration in Public Cloud
entry.content:
Hi,

Our customer was previously using RE-FX in S/4 for IFRS16 valuation and is moving to Public Cloud CLM.

We are in migration and cut-over phase and have migrated many contracts into CLM using the migration cockpit and simulated valuation successfully, but one contract generates a negative interest calculation error.

All the contracts were migrated with two statistical one-time conditions:

  • Asset Default Value - net worth value specified
  • Net present value (NPV) default - present value specified

For this lease contract the NPV value is > Contract value (value of the future lease payments considered for IFRS16).

Net Present Value Specified = 96,898.99

Contract Value = 76,762.75

Asset Default Value = 70,533.74 (ROU asset NBV)

 

When entering the valuation parameters, the interest rate entered is ignored due to the NPV condition value being specified.  As per SAP note, 3041660 the system calculates this interest rate automatically, based on NPV.

3041660 - 'Interest Rate' under 'Valuation Parameters' tab is different from valuation - SAP for Me

 

The error encountered during valuation is exactly as stipulated in this sap note, 3531206:

3531206 - RE-FX: RECEPR000, RECEPR116, RECEPR151, RECEPR502, RECEPR503, RECEPR506 when the Present Value Specified is used - SAP for Me

As per SAP note this error occurs where the present value exceeds the contract value, leading to an incorrect interest calculation based on a negative amount.  The Present Value Specified amount must be equal to or less than the contract value. 

 

Question – would you have any suggestions as to how to approach or resolve this migration issue?

 

Thanks

Sonya

 Other notes I looked at:

3626019 - Valuation Parameters negative interest - SAP for Me

3653143 - Central KBA: Interest Calculation in RE-FX - SAP for Me

entry.title: Guidance Required On Mapping Bankgiro and Plusgiro Payment Methods for Swedish Operations
entry.content:
As part of our Accounts Payable process for our Swedish operations, we are required to maintain Bankgiro and Plusgiro account numbers for our suppliers. However, we need clarification on the following:

  1. Where exactly should the Bankgiro and Plusgiro numbers be maintained in the Supplier Master Data — specifically, which fields under the Payment Transactions tab (Company Code level or General Data level) are designated for this purpose?

  2. How should the mapping be configured — is there a specific format, field assignment, or partner bank type that needs to be used to differentiate between Bankgiro and Plusgiro accounts?

  3. Are there any prerequisite configurations (e.g., bank key setup, payment method configuration, or house bank settings) that need to be in place before mapping these account numbers?

  4. Does this mapping require any specific country-specific settings for Sweden (Country Key: SE) to be activated?


------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------------
Learn more about the SAP Support user and program here.

entry.title: Re: Guidance Required On Mapping Bankgiro and Plusgiro Pa...
entry.content:
1. Where exactly should the Bankgiro and Plusgiro numbers be maintained in the Supplier Master Data — specifically, which fields under the Payment Transactions tab (Company Code level or General Data level) are designated for this purpose?

-->Financial Accounting Data (such as payment methods, reconciliation account) is entered at the company code level, while bank account details are maintained at the General Data level because they represent the supplier's banking information, which is typically shared across all company codes that transact with that supplier. 

2. How should the mapping be configured — is there a specific format, field assignment, or partner bank type that needs to be used to differentiate between Bankgiro and Plusgiro accounts?

>>The two methods can be differentiated via Bank Key

3. Are there any prerequisite configurations (e.g., bank key setup, payment method configuration, or house bank settings) that need to be in place before mapping these account numbers?

>>Yes. More information can be found in the Help Bank and Payment Operations | SAP Help Portal

4. Does this mapping require any specific country-specific settings for Sweden (Country Key: SE) to be activated?

>>Yes, it is outlined in the SAP Help Portal:- Sweden | SAP Help Portal

entry.title: Re: Automatic Segment Derivation from Cost Center in Journal Entries (SAP S/4HANA Public Cloud)
entry.content:
Hi, 
You wrote: "Cost centers are properly maintained." 
Does this include that there is also a profit center assigned to the cost center?
Because the derivation runs from

  • Line Item -> Controlling object (in your case a cost center)  in the line item
  • Cost center -> Profit Center
  • Profit center -> Segment

In case that the line item is not populated with a cost object (e.g. a supplier creditor) then document splitting will provide the additional entries, if customized and activated.

Best regards,

Wolf

entry.title: Re: SAP Public Cloud Quality Notification Action Box Issue
entry.content:
Good day @Totchiki25 

Thank you for your question.

The issue is that ME21N is a classic transaction based UI, therefore GUI transactions are not available in Public Cloud. In Public Cloud purchasing is exposed as the Create Purchase Order-Advances app, not as a GUI transaction you can freely call through a QM Action Box shortcut.

So, if you configured:

Follow up action = ZXXPO and Action Box entry = Q2-Create Purchase Order, the Action Box will not open ME21N.

I suggest you use the purchase oder app direct. Alternatively you may consider redesigning your follow up process. Instead of trying to jump from QM Action Box to ME21N, use a supported Public Cloud process such as:

  • Creating a follow up task in the notification
  • Navigating manually to the purchase order app, or
  • Designing a side-by-side extension if you require a guided handoff.

See the following references for more details.

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/28f492016a52485784d85b7a31f7542d.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/0e602d466b99490187fcbb30d1dc897c/65ebf13a182047e48ca59deff43eb325.html?locale=en-US

https://help.sap.com/docs/SAP_S4HANA_CLOUD/d1e58be39d884a0dbf75a7526a9acbf4/3bd1f43423b8495ba4b99ccd7a3ee29f.html?locale=en-US

Let me know if you have further questions.

Best regards

Chris

entry.title: Re: SAP Joule skill throwing byte_limit_size_exception
entry.content:
Answering my own question. 

The Context limit for Joule Skill's Output processing is 10KB. If you set the Joule skill output to be interpreted by Joule option and then your return value from "End" block is greater than 10KB, this error will come. 

To bypass this, disable the "Allow Joule to Interpret the result" toggle option in the Joule Skill Trigger Node. Then use a "Send Response" Node before the End block to show a custom output to the user. The "End" block can still return the data to other calling skills or Agents.

entry.title: Re: Batch management at 'Plant level and 'Material level'...
entry.content: You don’t need to activate plant-level batch management if material-level is already active. Doing so only makes the plant field available in MSC1N, but the uniqueness and characteristics of the batch remain controlled at material level. The plant flag is redundant in this setup, and its purpose is mainly to support scenarios where batch management is not enforced globally.

entry.title: Building and securing MCP servers in Python on BTP
entry.content:
MCP (Model Context Protocol) is the standard for tool access in AI agents. As SAP developers build extensions and integrations on SAP BTP, creating MCP servers has become equally relevant to expose business functionalities to AI agents. Whether you're extending Joule Studio or building custom AI solutions, MCP servers provide a standardized way to make your BTP services AI-accessible. In this tutorial, I'll show you how to build a production-ready MCP server on SAP Cloud Foundry using Python.

Scenario

We'll create three progressively complex MCP servers:
1. Simple Math Server - Understanding the basics
2. Research Server - Integrating Perplexity AI via Generative AI Hub
3. Secured Research Server - Adding IAS authentication

All running on SAP Cloud Foundry, one of the most flexible and cost-effective runtime options on BTP.

Prerequisites

Before starting, ensure you have:
- SAP BTP account with Cloud Foundry environment
- Cloud Foundry CLI installed and configured
- Python 3.9+ installed locally
- SAP Generative AI Hub access with service key (for Perplexity example)
- SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS) tenant with admin access (for authentication example)

Example 1: Simple MCP Server

Let's start with the basics. We'll use the fastmcp library which handles MCP's protocol specifications elegantly.

from starlette.responses import JSONResponse
from fastmcp import FastMCP
import os

# Initialize FastMCP server
mcp = FastMCP("Simple Math Server")


@mcp.tool()
def add(a: float, b: float) -> float:
    """
    Add two numbers together.

    Args:
        a: First number
        b: Second number

    Returns:
        Sum of a and b
    """
    return a + b


if __name__ == "__main__":
    mcp.run(transport="http", host="0.0.0.0", port=int(os.environ.get("PORT", 8080)))

Key BTP Adaptations:
- PORT environment variable: Cloud Foundry assigns a dynamic port via the PORT environment variable — we must use it
- HTTP transport: We use HTTP transport (not stdio) to make the MCP server accessible via network

To deploy, we need the manifest.yaml and a requirements.txt file. In addition I like to use a runtime.txt to specify a later python version.

---
applications:
- name: simple-mcp-server
  memory: 1024M
  disk_quota: 1024M
  buildpack: python_buildpack
  command: python server.py

Deploy to Cloud Foundry:

cd mcp_simple
cf push

That's it! You now have a working MCP server on BTP.

Example 2: Research Server with Perplexity AI

Now let's build something more practical: a research tool powered by Perplexity AI through SAP Generative AI Hub.

Why wrap Perplexity instead of using it directly?

You might wonder why not use Perplexity's native MCP server. Two reasons:
1. Educational value - Great exercise to understand the full stack
2. Generative AI Hub integration - The Hub exposes the API but not API keys. Perplexity's native MCP requires direct API keys, which isn't compatible with the Hub's proxy model where credentials stay on the server side.

Perplexity Client Implementation

This client handles:
- Loading AI Core credentials from ai_core_key.json
- OAuth token management with automatic refresh
- Calling Perplexity's sonar model via Generative AI Hub
- Formatting results with citations and related questions

Here's the core implementation snippet showing the Gen AI Hub integration:

class PerplexityClient:
    """Client for Perplexity API via SAP Gen AI Hub native proxy."""

    def __init__(self, config_file: str = 'ai_core_key.json'):
        """Initialize the client with AI Core credentials."""
        self.config = self._load_config(config_file)
        self.token = None
        self.token_expiry = 0
        self._refresh_token()

        # IMPORTANT: Create a Gen AI Hub deployment for Perplexity's sonar-pro model
        # Replace this with your own deployment ID from AI Core
        self.deployment_id = "d0229dc9c4b56704"

        # Build API URL: Gen AI Hub base URL + deployment endpoint
        self.base_url = self.config['serviceurls']['AI_API_URL']
        self.api_url = f"{self.base_url}/v2/inference/deployments/{self.deployment_id}/chat/completions"

    def research(self, query: str) -> str:
        """Research a query using Perplexity AI with real-time web search."""
        self._ensure_valid_token()

        payload = {
            "model": "sonar-pro",
            "messages": [{"role": "user", "content": query}],
            "max_tokens": 5000,
            "temperature": 0.3,
            "return_citations": True
        }

        headers = {
            "Authorization": f"Bearer {self.token}",
            "Content-Type": "application/json",
            "AI-Resource-Group": "default"
        }

        response = requests.post(self.api_url, json=payload, headers=headers)
        # ... format results with citations ...

 

The key is the URL structure: {AI_API_URL}/v2/inference/deployments/{deployment_id}/chat/completions — this routes through Gen AI Hub to Perplexity's native API while preserving citation metadata.

Note: You'll need to create a deployment in AI Core for the Perplexity model and update the deployment_id in the code with your own.

MCP Server with Research Tool

from starlette.responses import JSONResponse
from fastmcp import FastMCP
import os
from perplexity import PerplexityClient

# Initialize FastMCP server
mcp = FastMCP("Research Server")

# Initialize Perplexity client
client = PerplexityClient()


@mcp.tool()
def research(query: str) -> str:
    """
    Research tool using Perplexity AI with real-time web search and citations.

    Args:
        query: The research query (simple string input)

    Returns:
        Research results with answer and source citations
    """
    return client.research(query)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    mcp.run(transport="http", host="0.0.0.0", port=int(os.environ.get("PORT", 8080)))

Simple and clean — the MCP decorator handles all protocol complexity, and we just delegate the query to our Perplexity client.

Important: We're using Generative AI Hub's native Perplexity proxy (not the orchestration module) because it preserves citation metadata that's crucial for research tasks.

Example 3: Adding IAS Authentication

For production use, we need to secure the MCP server. Let's add authentication using SAP Cloud Identity Services (IAS).

IAS Setup Prerequisites

Before implementing authentication, complete these IAS configuration steps:
1. Create an IAS application in your IAS tenant
2. Configure OAuth 2.0 client credentials
3. Add custom attribute ias_apis with value api_read_access
4. Note your token URL, client ID, client secret, and audience

For detailed IAS setup instructions, refer to my previous blog post on Python authentication with IAS.

Secured MCP Server Implementation

The secured version adds an authentication middleware that:
- Validates JWT tokens from IAS
- Verifies token signature using JWKS (JSON Web Key Set)
- Checks audience and issuer claims
- Ensures required scope (api_read_access) is present

from fastmcp import FastMCP
from fastapi import FastAPI, Request
import os
import requests
import jwt
import uvicorn
from perplexity import PerplexityClient
from fastapi.responses import JSONResponse
from starlette.middleware.base import BaseHTTPMiddleware

# Initialize Perplexity client
client = PerplexityClient()

# Create MCP server
mcp = FastMCP("Research Server")

# IAS Configuration - Replace with your values
ISSUER = "https://your-tenant.accounts.ondemand.com"
JWKS_URL = f"{ISSUER}/oauth2/certs"
AUDIENCE = "your-client-id-here"


class IASAuthMiddleware(BaseHTTPMiddleware):
    """Middleware to validate IAS JWT tokens."""

    def get_public_key(self, token: str):
        """Fetch JWKS and find matching public key for token validation."""
        kid = jwt.get_unverified_header(token)["kid"]
        jwks = requests.get(JWKS_URL).json()

        for key in jwks["keys"]:
            if key["kid"] == kid:
                return jwt.algorithms.RSAAlgorithm.from_jwk(key)

        raise Exception("No matching key found in JWKS")

    def verify_token(self, token: str):
        """Validate JWT token signature and claims."""
        public_key = self.get_public_key(token)
        payload = jwt.decode(
            token,
            public_key,
            algorithms=["RS256"],
            audience=AUDIENCE,
            issuer=ISSUER
        )

        # Verify required scope
        if "api_read_access" not in payload.get("ias_apis", []):
            raise Exception("Missing required ias_apis scope")

        return payload

    async def dispatch(self, request: Request, call_next):
        # Extract and validate authorization header
        auth_header = request.headers.get("Authorization")

        if not auth_header or not auth_header.startswith("Bearer "):
            return JSONResponse(
                status_code=401,
                content={"detail": "Missing or invalid authorization header"}
            )

        token = auth_header.split(" ")[1]

        try:
            payload = self.verify_token(token)
            request.state.user = payload
        except Exception as e:
            return JSONResponse(
                status_code=401,
                content={"detail": f"Authentication failed: {str(e)}"}
            )

        return await call_next(request)


@mcp.tool()
def research(query: str) -> str:
    """
    Research tool using Perplexity AI with real-time web search and citations.

    Args:
        query: The research query

    Returns:
        Research results with answer and source citations
    """
    return client.research(query)


# Create ASGI app from MCP server
mcp_app = mcp.http_app(path='/mcp')

# Create FastAPI wrapper with lifespan management
app = FastAPI(title="Researcher MCP", lifespan=mcp_app.lifespan)

# Add IAS authentication middleware
mcp_app.add_middleware(IASAuthMiddleware)

# Mount the MCP server
app.mount("/", mcp_app)


if __name__ == "__main__":
    uvicorn.run(app, host="0.0.0.0", port=int(os.environ.get("PORT", 8080)))

What changed for authentication:

The key difference from the unsecured version is the middleware layer. Since fastmcp is built on Starlette/FastAPI, we can leverage standard ASGI middleware patterns. The IASAuthMiddleware intercepts every request, validates the JWT token against IAS's public keys (JWKS), and checks required claims before allowing access to MCP endpoints.

To integrate the middleware, we extract the ASGI app from fastmcp using http_app(), wrap it with FastAPI to manage lifespan properly, add our authentication middleware, and mount it. This pattern works seamlessly with Cloud Foundry's router.

Testing Authentication

Test your secured endpoint:

1. Without token (should fail):
curl "https://research-mcp-server-ias-auth.cfapps.sap.hana.ondemand.com/mcp"
Response: 401 - {"detail":"Missing or invalid authorization header"}

2. With valid token (should succeed):
curl "https://research-mcp-server-ias-auth.cfapps.sap.hana.ondemand.com/mcp" \
  -H "Authorization: Bearer eyJqa3U..."
Response: 200 OK with mcp-session-id header

Perfect! Our MCP server now requires valid IAS authentication.

Using the MCP Server in Joule Studio

Now that we have a secured MCP server, let's integrate it with Joule Studio to build an AI agent with research capabilities.

Step 1: Create a Destination

Navigate to your BTP Subaccount (where Joule Studio is running):
1. Go to Connectivity → Destinations
2. Create a new destination with these settings:
- Name: research-mcp-server
- Type: HTTP
- URL: https://research-mcp-server-ias-auth.cfapps.sap.hana.ondemand.com
- Authentication: OAuth2ClientCredentials
- Client ID: Your IAS client ID
- Client Secret: Your IAS client secret
- Token Service URL: https://your-tenant.accounts.ondemand.com/oauth2/token

Additional Properties are needed for Joule Studio to recognize the Destination accordingly:

sap-joule-studio-mcp-server: true
sap.processautomation.enabled: true

Important: Don't include /mcp in the URL — Joule Studio appends this automatically.

Now the Destination again needs to be added to the respective environment of SAP Build Process Automation via the Control Tower > Destinations.


Step 2: Create Agent in Joule Studio

 

1. Create a new Agent in your Joule Extension Project
2. Define the agent's expertise and instructions
3. In the MCP section, click Add MCP Server
4. Select your research-mcp-server destination
5. Verify the /mcp path (default is fine)
6. You'll see the available tools (in our case: research)

 


Step 3: Test Your Agent

Start a conversation with your agent and ask it to research current information:

"Research the current situation in the Middle East"

The agent now has access to real-time web information via your MCP server!

 


This opens powerful possibilities: combine up-to-date web data with transactional business data from S/4HANA, SuccessFactors, or other SAP systems.

Using It in Action

Now let's test it in action - I started the agent and asked it to research the current situation in the Middle East and it works! Up-to-date information directly in Joule - can now be combined with any business transactional data one might pull from an S/4HANA system.

By the way, check out my blog on connecting Joule Studio to on-premises HTTP APIs to extend this pattern further.

In the GitHub repository I've added all the different examples including the simple example, with auth based on IAS as well as based on XSUAA, and the Perplexity code. Hope you found this interesting. Leave a comment in case of any questions!

entry.title: Re: How to use BTP Destination with REST API in UI5 App (...
entry.content:
@TimurKalynovskyi 

Looking at your xs-app.json, the rewrite rule strips /backend before forwarding to the destination:

"source": "^/backend/(.*)$",
"target": "/$1"

This means your actual backend API starts with /application/..., not /backend/application/.... The /backend prefix is only used locally as a routing marker.

The issue is that fiori-tools-proxy forwards the full path including /backend to the destination, while xs-app.json removes it. That mismatch causes the 404 locally.

The simplest fix is to remove the /backend prefix entirely and align both configurations to use the real API path directly:

ui5.yaml:

server:
  customMiddleware:
    - name: fiori-tools-proxy
      afterMiddleware: compression
      configuration:
        ignoreCertErrors: false
        ui5:
          path:
            - /resources
            - /test-resources
          url: https://ui5.sap.com
        backend:
          - path: /application
            destination: TEST_BACKEND

xs-app.json:

{
  "source": "^/application/(.*)$",
  "target": "/$1",
  "destination": "TEST_BACKEND",
  "authenticationType": "none"
}

Controller:

url: "/application/api/v1/documenthub/$metadata"

With this approach, both local (fiori-tools-proxy) and deployed (App Router via xs-app.json) environments forward requests consistently to the backend, and no path rewriting is needed in either configuration.

last updated: 2026-03-26

Introduction
The article explains the SAP GUI – TCODE (Transaction Code): DBACOCKPIT (SQL Plan Cache) usage in details.

SAP GUI

  • SAP Help Portal – SAP GUI for Windows
  • SAP Community – SAP GUI – SAP GUI Family
  • 147519 – Maintenance strategy / deadlines for SAP GUI for Windows / SAP GUI for Java

Description
The SQL plan cache provides an overview of compiled queries.

Transaction Code:
DBACOCKPIT

Performance > SQL Plan Cache

entry.title: Re: BP Creation – Role Grouping Concept
entry.content:
Group the Company view, Purchasing view under a single role. This enables users to access all views within one role, rather than selecting separate roles for each individual view.

1. DEFINE VENDOR ACCOUNT GROUP:

SPRO->FINANCIAL ACCOUNTING -> ACCOUNTS RECEIVABLE AND PAYABLE -> SUPPLIER ACCOUNTS -> MASTER DATA -> DEFINE ACCOUNT GRP WITH SCREEN LAYOUT.

2. CONFIGURE BP GROUPING:

CROSS APPLICATION COMPONENT -> SAP BUSINESS PARTNER -> BUSINESS PARTNER -> BASIC SETTINGS -> NUMBER RANGES AND GROUPING -> DEFINE GROUPINGS AND ASSIGN NUMBER RANGE.

3. ASSIGNMENT OF BP GROUPING TO VENDOR ACCOUNT GROUP

MASTER DATA SYNCHRONIZATION -> CUSTOMER/VENDOR INTEGRATION -> BUSINESS PARTNER SETTINGS -> SETTINGS FOR VENDOR INTEGRATION -> FIELD ASSIGNMENT OF VENDOR INTEGRATION -> ASSIGN KEYS -> DIRECTION BP TO VENDOR.

If Same Nos. is not checked then BP and SUPPLIER number will not be same.

4. BP role Grouping:

CROSS-APPLICATION COMPONENT -> SAP BUSINESS PARTNER -> BUSINESS PARTNER -> BUSINESS PARTNER ROLE -> DEFINE BP ROLE GROUPINGS

By assigning this BP role to this BP role group, in the screen the role assigned to that group will be displayed.

entry.title: Questions: Relationship between CBAR (Accelerated Return) and Quantity Contracts in S/4HANA
entry.content:
Hi people, 
I'd greatly appreciate your input on the matter.

Summary of the issue

Our process is the following:
1. Normal CQ Contract > SO > DEL > Invoice (F2)
2. We've invoiced the customer too high a price, so we create CBAR (for the full price) w. ref to F2, with selections: 

  • Return Reason: Customer invoiced too much (too high price)
  • Follow-Up Act.: Material Remains at customer site
  • Refund Type: Credit Memo
  • Refund Control: P- Refund Allowed

3. We issue a Debit Memo Request (Type: DR) w. ref to the F2 document for the correct price the customer owes us.

We have an issue that concerns the impact of Advanced Returns Management (ARM) CBAR documents on quantity contracts. In the tested scenario, a sales order created with reference to a quantity contract correctly consumes quantity from the contract, but as soon as a CBAR is created with reference to the billing document, the previously referenced quantity is reopened in the contract. This happens even in cases where the CBAR is used only for a value correction such as a price adjustment, with no physical return of goods and material remaining at the customer site.

In practice, this means that the contract open quantity increases again already at CBAR creation, while the later debit memo request/debit memo documents do not reduce the quantity again. As a result, the quantity contract no longer reflects the actual situation correctly. From a business perspective, this is problematic because the original quantity was still sold and should remain consumed; only the value was corrected.

Our findings indicate that the behaviour is most likely linked to the fact that the CBAR item category RENV is configured as a returns item, and SAP appears to treat it as a returns-relevant follow-on document in the contract reference chain. This aligns to some extent with SAP Note 3604002, where SAP confirms that creating a return order for a sales order that referenced a quantity contract will reopen the contract quantity. However, it remains unclear whether SAP explicitly intended the same behavior for ARM CBAR scenarios that are purely value-related.

1. Initial Test Results

Step Document Refund Type Refund Control Follow-up Act. Open Qty in Contract Comment

1F2: 9000001433; 24 PAA---124Baseline
2CBAR: 60000731; 6 PAAReplacement MaterialImmediatelyReceive into Plant130CBAR affects quantity
3CBAR: 60000733; 2 PAACredit MemoDecide LaterReceive into Plant132SDF delivery does not impact, but CBAR impacts quantity

These tests show that the contract quantity is already affected at the point of CBAR creation. The later SDF-related flow does not appear to be the trigger (as per SAP note 3666149)

2. Test results after removing “Sched. Line Allowed”

When the “Sched. Line Allowed”  (due to testing SAP Note 610629) check was removed and a CBAR was tested again, the quantity was no longer being properly registered and it was not possible to create a complete CBAR. The change was therefore reverted.

It should also be noted that item category G2W in the Credit Memo Request already has this check unselected.

The F1 help text states:

“Items that are relevant for delivery, such as sales order items, can always have schedule lines. On the other hand, items that are not relevant for delivery, such as items in contracts and credit memo requests, do not have schedule lines.”

From this, it appears that RENV needs to remain delivery-relevant due to the possible Replacement/Credit Memo reference types, which may explain why simply removing the check is not a viable solution.

3. SAP notes and documentation reviewed

  • SAP Note 3604002 states that when a return order is created for a sales order that referenced a quantity contract, the contract is opened again, and SAP confirms that this is correct system behavior, because the returned quantity is made available again for new reference.
  • We did not find official SAP documentation explicitly confirming whether this is also intended for ARM/CBAR scenarios, especially where the case is purely value-related.
  • SAP Note 3666149 was the only relevant note found regarding SDF, and it supports that the SDF (FOC) delivery should not affect open/referenced quantities. By that same logic, the returns delivery might also be expected not to be the trigger in this scenario, since the quantity change already happens at CBAR creation.
  • From SAP Note 610629, we can only assume that the “Sched. Line Allowed” flag may play some role in the observed behaviour, but it remains unclear what SAP intended specifically in S/4HANA with quantity contracts and ARM flows.
  • The same note also says:

“Exclusively for Fashion-relevant contracts (LO-FSH), however, you can define that requirements can be created from contracts as well.”

At this stage, it is unclear how this statement should be interpreted in the context of this ARM scenario.

4. Current logical interpretation

The most logical explanation at present is that SAP sees the CBAR and the follow-on Credit Memo Request as an indication that there is an issue with the original quantity flow, regardless of whether the case is truly quantity-related or only value-related. In other words, SAP may be interpreting the presence of the returns document itself as a reason to reopen the quantity contract.

Under that interpretation, the likely impact on contracts would be:
Documents that appear to have an impact: Sales Order; CBAR, with or without a returns delivery
Documents that appear not to have an impact: Credit Memo Request/Credit Memo; Debit Memo Request/Debit Memo; SDF (FOC delivery); potentially the Returns Delivery itself

5. Open questions

Business/design:

  • Should a value-only CBAR affect a quantity contract?
  • Should the contract quantity stay consumed if there is no physical return and only a price correction?
  • Is this intended SAP behavior or just a side effect of using a returns item category?

SAP standard behaviour:

  • Does SAP Note 3604002 also apply to ARM/CBAR, or only to classic return orders?
  • Is there any official SAP documentation saying whether value-only CBARs should reopen quantity contracts?
  • What is SAP’s intended standard behavior for quantity contracts in ARM flows?

Config/technical: 

  • Is the behavior mainly caused by RENV, Sched. Line Allowed, or the standard reference/document flow?
  • Is there an SAP-supported way to keep RENV for ARM, but stop it from reopening the contract in value-only cases?
  • Which ARM follow-on documents are actually meant to affect quantity contracts?

Process:

  • Should price corrections for quantity-contract-based sales use a different process instead of CBAR?
  • If SAP standard cannot separate true returns from value-only corrections, is a custom solution needed?

I know this is a tad long, but would greatly appreciate some input on the matter.

entry.title: Re: SAP Build Apps Deprecation and The Path Forward
entry.content:
I understand that a product is discontinued when there isn’t enough demand for it on the market. But what’s surprising is how suddenly it’s being discontinued.
Where have all those citizen developers suddenly gone—the very people the product was aimed at?
Of course, the IT world moves very quickly, but customers don’t have the time or the budget to constantly train their teams on new tools or migrate applications.
What’s up next? The discontinuation of MDK or SAP Build Code? According to the latest usage statistics from the survey, SAP Build Code is also used very little.
SAP’s goal should actually be to quickly bring ONE technology to market that is customer-friendly in terms of pricing and feature set, and then support it for 10 years—not to develop overlapping tools in the same segment in parallel, some of which were initially unattractive to customers due to their pricing.

entry.title: Re: Standard SAP MB5S throwing TIME OUT ERROR
entry.content:
Hi,

The TIME_OUT occurs mainly due to:

1. Large data selection

MB5S reads:

Purchase orders

GR/IR balances

Material documents

FI postings

A wide date range or missing filters can easily exceed the dialog timeout limit.

2. Standard SAP timeout limit

SAP dialog work processes typically have a timeout of:

  • 600 seconds (10 mins)
  • or sometimes 1200 seconds (20 mins)

MB5S often exceeds this in dialog mode.

3. System performance or indexing issues

If indexing or buffering is slow, MB5S becomes worse.

 

Fix.

MB5S can absolutely be run in background. Many companies do this.

Steps:

  1. Go to MB5S.
  2. Enter selection parameters.
  3. Click Program → Execute in Background.
  4. Choose output (e.g., Spool (List), or XLS).
  5. Schedule immediately or periodically.

 

But this is generally not recommended, as it affects entire system dialog behavior.

Only do this if absolutely required.

 

Reduce the data volume

Use filters such as:

Company code

Purchasing organization

Plant

Smaller dataset → faster MB5S → no TIME_OUT.

entry.title: odataVersion 4.01 prevents app from being imported into Work Zone HTML5 Apps
entry.content:
Hi community,

I have a UI5 app that uses an OData service, and the odataVersion in manifest.json is set to 4.01.

      "mainService": {
        "uri": "/odata/v4/quote/",
        "type": "OData",
        "settings": {
          "annotations": [
            "annotation"
          ],
          "localUri": "localService/mainService/metadata.xml",
          "odataVersion": "4.01"
        }
      }

When I deploy this app to Cloud Foundry and refresh the Channel Manager in SAP Build Work Zone, the app fails to be imported, and the downloaded report contains the following error:

 
businessapp does not match the schema, dataPath: undefined, message: 'must be equal to one of the allowed values' [ '2.0', '4.0' ]"

Changing odataVersion to 4.0 makes the import succeed.

I believe this is a bug in Work Zone. I hope it will be fixed, but I am sharing this in case others are facing the same issue.

entry.title: Note 933420 is incorrect about ALSM_EXCEL_TO_INTERNAL_TABLE limitations
entry.content:
The note 933420 - ALSM_EXCEL_TO_INTERNAL_TABLE - SAP for Me is misleading:

  • The output structure is limited to 9999 rows and columns.

I trust the indications given in this question/answer ALSM_EXCEL_TO_INTERNAL_TABLE confusion - SAP Community:

  • nearly 70.000 rows
  • 256 columns...?

In fact, to understand why the note says "9999", or what it means, one have to look at ALSM_EXCEL_TO_INTERNAL_TABLE, it returns an internal table (parameter INTERN) with one line per value, which has this structure (ALSMEX_TABLINE):

  • ROW (4 digits)
  • COL (4 digits)
  • VALUE (50 characters)

So, ROW and COL may have a value ranging from 0 to 9999, starting from 1 for the first row.

What the note doesn't say, is that it doesn't stop returning the Excel values at the row 9999, if more lines exist in the Excel file they are returned, the ROW number is 0 for the line # 10.000, 1 for the line # 10.001 and so on.

NB: the note says that you may copy the function module to a custom one and change the type of the internal table ALSMEX_TABLINE to a custom type with the three components ROW, COL and VALUE but define them with an increased length as per your requirements.

Do you have any precise information about the real limitations other than what said here? (and possibly why it's limited to around 70000 and 256)

Thanks.

Sandra

NB: I know this function module is not supported by SAP and should not be used (it's also what the note says).

entry.title: Re: Spare Parts Discount Based on Service Contract Value...
entry.content:
Hi Adarsh,

You can update the price agreements in the service contract at the line item level for a specific spare part with 20% discount. When a service order is created in reference to the service contract, and if you include the spare part then the 20% discount will be automatically updated in the service order.

The discounts can be set at price agreements section for a specific material or group of materials using material group attribute.

Regards,

Siraj

entry.title: Re: logo condition issue in adobe forms
entry.content:
found a fix using javascript, not sure if it translates to formcalc

if ($record.HEADER.DS_EKKO.BUKRS.value != "C020") this.presence = "hidden";

a singular condition on the dark logo, which was missing the ".value" to retrieve the value of the data binding. put it on top of the white logo and now one will override the other on the layout if the condition is correct.

entry.title: Re: Supplier rebate / allowance scenario in SAP S/4HANA C...
entry.content:
Please find below answers to your questions

1) Is this the correct standard approach in S/4HANA Cloud Public Edition?
Yes — the recommended way is to use Settlement Management with supplier condition contracts. This is the standard functionality for supplier rebates, allowances, and fixed‑amount agreements. It supports both value‑based and quantity‑based conditions, and settlement runs generate the credit notes or postings back to the supplier account.

2) Can the agreement logic be maintained by supplier + product / product group / hierarchy level?

Yes you can maintain agreements at different levels:

  • Supplier + specific material
  • Supplier + material group / hierarchy node
  • Supplier + higher product category

The condition contract allows you to define the scope (materials, groups, or hierarchies) and link it to the supplier. This way, Supplier A and Supplier B can have different rules for the same product group.

3) For fixed monthly amounts like display support, is it usually handled as part of the same supplier settlement logic, or as a separate process?
These can also be handled in Settlement Management as fixed‑amount conditions within the supplier contract. Alternatively, if the allowance is not tied to purchase volumes, some companies treat it as a periodic accrual/credit memo outside of settlement. Best practice is to keep it in the same supplier condition contract so that all supplier‑related rebates and allowances are tracked consistently.

4) If part of the agreement depends on our sales to customers instead of only purchasing-related data, is that supported in standard Public Cloud?
Supplier condition contracts are purchase‑side only. They track procurement volumes/values and fixed allowances. What is not standard is a supplier rebate that depends directly on your sell‑out data (i.e., “Supplier pays you back if you sell X units to your customers”). That cross‑link between supplier agreements and customer sales is not delivered out‑of‑the‑box in Public Cloud.

5) Tracking & Settlement
With this setup, you can clearly track:

  • What you pay each supplier (via purchase invoices).
  • What you should receive back (rebates, fixed amounts, credits).
  • How it is settled (automated settlement run generates supplier credit notes or postings).

entry.title: Re: Batch management at 'Plant level and 'Material level'...
entry.content:
1) Material‑level Batch Management (Global)
If you activate batch management at material level (OMCE), the batch is unique across all plants. Example:

  • Material ABC has batch 1000.
  • Whether you are in Plant ZXZZ or ZYZZ, batch 1000 refers to the same global batch record.

In this case, you don’t need the plant flag; the system enforces batch management everywhere automatically.

2) Plant‑level Batch Management (Local)
If you don’t activate material‑level batch management, then the plant flag (MARC‑XCHPF) becomes relevant. Example:

  • Plant ZXZZ: Material ABC has batch management active.
  • Plant ZYZZ: Material ABC does not have batch management active.

Here, batch management applies only in ZXZZ. You can create batches in ZXZZ but not in ZYZZ. This allows flexibility if some plants manage batches and others don’t.

3) Why the Plant flag is redundant when material‑level is Active
Once you switch on material‑level batch management, every plant must manage batches for that material. The plant flag then just mirrors the global setting; it doesn’t add new functionality. Example:

  • Material ABC has material‑level batch management active.
  • Even if MARC‑XCHPF is unticked in Plant ZYZZ, the system still enforces batch management globally.

So the plant flag doesn’t change behavior; it only makes the plant field visible in MSC1N. Having said that the plant flag’s purpose is to support local batch management when global (material‑level) is not enforced. Once global is active, the plant flag is effectively redundant.

entry.title: Advanced Returns Management (CBAR) and Quantity Contracts
entry.content:
Hi all, I’d appreciate your input on the following issue.

We have process where the flow is:
1. Quantity Contract > Sales Order > Delivery > Invoice (F2)
2. The customer was invoiced too high a price, so we create a CBAR with reference to the F2 document for a value correction only, using:

  • Return Reason: Customer invoiced too much
  • Follow-Up Activity: Material Remains at Customer Site
  • Refund Type: Credit Memo
  • Refund Control: Refund Allowed

3. We then create a Debit Memo Request (type DR) with reference to the F2 document for the correct price the customer actually owes.

Issue: When the sales order is created with reference to the quantity contract, the contract quantity is consumed correctly. However, once the CBAR is created with reference to the billing document, the contract quantity is reopened. This happens even though there is no physical return and the material remains at the customer site.

The later debit memo process does not consume the quantity again, so the contract open quantity becomes incorrect. From a business perspective, this is problematic because the goods were still sold; only the price was corrected.

Findings: Our current assumption is that SAP treats the CBAR as returns-relevant because item category RENV is configured as a returns item. This seems similar to SAP Note 3604002, where SAP confirms that a return order created for a sales order referencing a quantity contract reopens the contract quantity. However, it is unclear whether SAP also intends this behavior for ARM CBAR scenarios that are value-only.

Tests: Initial tests showed that the contract quantity is already affected at CBAR creation.
The SDF / free-of-charge follow-on flow does not seem to be the trigger, which aligns with SAP Note 3666149.

We also tested removing the “Sched. Line Allowed” indicator (based on SAP Note 610629), but then the quantity was no longer registered properly and a complete CBAR could not be created. The change was reverted.
This suggests that simply removing schedule line relevance is not a viable solution for RENV.

Main question: Is it standard SAP behaviour that a value-only CBAR reopens quantity contract quantities, or is this just a side effect of the returns item category/document flow design?

Open points/We are mainly trying to understand:

  • Should a value-only CBAR affect a quantity contract at all?
  • Does SAP Note 3604002 also apply to ARM/CBAR, or only to classic return orders?
  • Is there any SAP-supported way to keep ARM standard behavior, but prevent contract quantity reopening in value-only cases?
  • If not, should price corrections for quantity-contract sales be handled with a different process?

Any input would be greatly appreciated.
If you need more detailed information, I've created this blog post as well: Questions: Relationship between CBAR (Accelerated Return) and Quantity Contracts in S/4HANA

entry.title: Re: Event Mesh + AMQP: The Retry Trap Nobody Warns You About
entry.content:
@RubikWal 
For individual reprocessing of DMQ messages best would be DataStore where you can pick specific Entry ID to reprocess. Therefore AMQP for standard handling & automatic retry + DataStore for DMQ. This is analogical for JMS (where DataStore is also perfect match). pls check great block from PipelineConcept:
https://community.sap.com/t5/integration-blog-posts/process-integration-pipeline-extension-restart-via-data-store/ba-p/14153116 

br, Mateusz

entry.title: Re: Currency Conversion in SAP Datasphere: The Next Level
entry.content:
Thank you for the blog post! Your approach is clever and provide flexibility for the users, which can decide the currency they want to see in their reports.

I wonder how the performance is when doing this currency conversion "on the fly" in the Analytic Model, especially with models having a lot of records. Do you have any insights regarding that?

For one of our customer, we implemented something slightly different, but that provides similar functionality. In a view in our propagation layer, we created several dedicated columns for amounts in various currencies (e.g. company currency, global currency, and fixed currencies such as USD and CAD), each with their corresponding currency column. We persist that view for performance purposes (this is actually the key for top performance in our case - especially when using the partitioning feature). In a subsequent view in the reporting layer, there are two "dynamic" columns, one for the amount, and the other for the currency. These two columns contain a simple "CASE.. WHEN.. END" formula based on an input parameter that selects the amount and currency columns based on the selection of the input parameter. The input parameter is passed to the Analytic Model, and only the two "dynamic" fields are shown in the report.

It is always interesting to share (and compare!) the different approach we are implementing to resolve similar problems.

Cheers! Alex
